articles.title: Tata Motors Unveils New Electric SUV with 500km Range
articles.body: Tata Motors has launched its latest electric SUV, the Nexon EV Max Plus, featuring an impressive 500-kilometer range on a single charge. The vehicle is priced competitively at Rs 18.5 lakh and comes with advanced driver assistance systems. Industry experts believe this launch will significantly boost Tata's market share in the rapidly growing EV segment. The company has invested over Rs 5,000 crore in developing this platform. The SUV features a 60 kWh battery pack and supports fast charging, reaching 80% charge in just 45 minutes. Tata Motors CEO stated that the company aims to achieve 25% of its total sales from EVs by 2027. The vehicle will compete directly with MG ZS EV and Hyundai Kona Electric. Bookings have already crossed 10,000 units within the first week of announcement. The company plans to expand its EV charging infrastructure across 100 cities in India. This launch comes at a time when the government is pushing for increased EV adoption through various incentives and subsidies.

articles.title: RBI Keeps Repo Rate Unchanged at 6.5%, Maintains Accommodative Stance
articles.body: The Reserve Bank of India has decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the third consecutive policy meeting. The Monetary Policy Committee voted 5-1 in favor of maintaining the status quo. RBI Governor emphasized that inflation remains within the target range of 4% with a tolerance band of +/- 2%. The central bank has revised its GDP growth forecast for FY2027 to 6.8% from the earlier estimate of 6.5%. Experts believe this decision will provide stability to the financial markets and support economic growth. The RBI also announced measures to enhance liquidity in the banking system. Foreign institutional investors have welcomed this decision, with the rupee strengthening against the dollar. The central bank highlighted that global economic uncertainties, particularly related to crude oil prices, remain a concern. Retail inflation stood at 4.2% in February 2026. The RBI maintained its accommodative stance to support growth while keeping inflation under control.

articles.title: Infosys Reports 15% YoY Growth in Q4 Revenue, Announces Rs 10,000 Crore Buyback
articles.body: Infosys has reported a strong 15% year-on-year growth in its Q4 FY2026 revenue, reaching Rs 42,500 crore. The company's net profit increased by 12% to Rs 7,800 crore. The board has approved a share buyback program worth Rs 10,000 crore at a maximum price of Rs 1,850 per share. CEO Salil Parekh highlighted that the company's digital transformation services continue to drive growth, accounting for 62% of total revenue. The company has also announced a final dividend of Rs 18 per share. Infosys added 85 new clients during the quarter and saw strong deal wins in the BFSI and retail sectors. The company's attrition rate has improved to 14.2% from 18.5% in the previous quarter. For FY2027, Infosys has guided for revenue growth of 10-12% in constant currency terms. The company is investing heavily in AI and generative AI capabilities to stay competitive. Employee headcount stood at 3.45 lakh at the end of Q4.

articles.title: Reliance Industries Reports Strong Q4 Performance, Expands Operations in Energy And Telecom Sectors
articles.body: Reliance Industries has announced robust financial results for Q4 FY2026, with revenue growing by 18% year-on-year. The company's strong performance was driven by increased demand in the energy and telecom sectors. Management highlighted that strategic investments in technology and infrastructure have started yielding positive results. The company plans to expand its operations across multiple geographies and invest heavily in research and development. Analysts remain bullish on the stock, citing strong fundamentals and growth prospects. The company has also announced plans to hire 5,000 employees in the coming quarter. Market share has increased by 3 percentage points compared to the previous year. The board has recommended a dividend of Rs 12 per share. Operating margins improved to 22% from 19% in the previous quarter. The company is well-positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the Indian market.
